content:
4 min read
Just now

--

If you’ve looked at your organic search traffic lately and felt a bit uneasy, you’re not the only one.
For two decades, the digital marketing approach was pretty straightforward: find a keyword that people search for a lot, write a long guide of around 2,000 words, fill it with related keywords, get some backlinks, and then wait for Google to bring people to your site.
But that time is now ending.
We’re moving into what’s called the Zero-Click Era.
With tools like AI Overviews, Perplexity, and ChatGPT, search engines are no longer just ways to find websites; they’re becoming the main places people get their answers. These engines scan the web, summarise what they find, and give the user the answer without needing them to click on anything.
This isn’t the end of search; it’s just a change.
To keep up, marketers and content creators need to split their strategies into two different approaches: one for the machines, called Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO), and one for people, called Human-in-the-Loop Content.
Here’s how to play the new game.
The Machine Feed (Generative Engine Optimisation):
Traditional SEO was about getting an algorithm to rank your link, but GEO is about getting a Large Language Model (LLM) to cite your brand as the source of truth…
